agent_raw:
The procurement recommendation is premature. The comparison between the incumbent and challenger is not apples-to-apples because three important conditions changed across the evaluation windows.

The incumbent was evaluated during a 24-month window that explicitly included a record cold snap in January 2022 and above-average summer peaks in both years. The challenger was evaluated during a 14-month window selected after the grid reliability stabilization program was complete — a period with materially fewer transmission outages and, by implication, a different mix of weather-driven demand conditions. These are not the same operating environment.

The outage regime changed materially between the two windows. The incumbent period included 23 unplanned transmission outages; the challenger period included only 7. Transmission outages affect dispatch patterns, load forecasting difficulty, and MAPE. The challenger was evaluated in an operationally easier environment.

The forecast information cutoff also differs. The incumbent submitted day-ahead forecasts by 16:00 on the prior business day. The challenger uses weather forecasts issued at 06:00 local time — more than 14 hours later than the incumbent's information cutoff. A model that has access to more recent weather information will naturally forecast more accurately. This timing advantage is not a model quality difference.

A matched rolling-origin bakeoff on the same calendar dates with the same weather-forecast cutoff and identical outage handling is required before a vendor selection is justified.
all_issues_raised: Weather regime mismatch: record cold snap and summer peaks vs post-stabilization period
Outage regime difference: 23 unplanned outages (incumbent) vs 7 (challenger)
Forecast information cutoff mismatch: 16:00 prior day (incumbent) vs 06:00 current day (challenger)

empirical_test.condition: Matched rolling-origin bakeoff on same calendar dates with same weather-forecast cutoff and identical outage-treatment rule
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Challenger MAPE edge shrinks below 0.2 points or reverses in major winter and summer regimes under same-date bakeoff
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Challenger beats incumbent by at least 0.7 MAPE points overall and within major weather regimes across at least one full year with normalized cutoff
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Challenger wins overall but only on milder days, or apparent edge disappears when both use same weather issuance time
